headlines.title: How war on Iran changed the global energy sector forever
headlines.snippet: A war on Iran is expected to permanently alter the global energy sector, accelerating the shift to renewable energy sources and prompting a search for alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz, according to energy analysts.

headlines.title: After Afghanistan fires drones into Pakistan, what’s next?
headlines.snippet: Following Afghanistan's drone strikes into Pakistan, the two nations find themselves in an ongoing cycle of cross-border attacks, severely damaging their relationship.

headlines.title: Caught between the US and China, can Europe remain competitive?
headlines.snippet: Europe is navigating growing trade tensions by attempting to maintain its competitiveness while balancing its relationships with both the United States and China.

headlines.title: Colorado primary election results: Melat Kiros wins – key takeaways
headlines.snippet: Melat Kiros has won the Colorado primary election, defeating Diana DeGette, who had served for as long as Kiros has been alive.

headlines.title: Algeria heads to legislative polls amid record-low turnout fear
headlines.snippet: Algeria is heading to legislative polls with fears of a record-low voter turnout due to post-Hirak distrust, candidate exclusions, and political apathy.

headlines.title: Israel arrests five women activists in the occupied West Bank
headlines.snippet: The Israeli army arrested five women activists, who work with health committees, in the occupied West Bank after raiding their homes. The arrests included Jamila Abu Dahou, Jamila Kanaan, Maysar al-Faqih, Faten Hanaysheh, and Etaf Bader, among other Palestinians.

headlines.title: Lebanon death toll from Israeli attacks reaches 4,297
headlines.snippet: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have resulted in 4,297 deaths and 12,196 injuries since March 2, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

headlines.title: State-backed UAE and Bahrain teams under scrutiny ahead of Tour de France
headlines.snippet: Ahead of the Tour de France, state-backed UAE and Bahrain cycling teams are under scrutiny, with cycling authorities urged to suspend their licenses due to human rights concerns.

headlines.title: Israeli press review: Netanyahu says wars will never end
headlines.snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel's wars across the Middle East will never end. Meanwhile, polls indicate a rise in racism among Israeli students and a high incidence of PTSD in children.

headlines.title: Exclusive: British Museum made false claims about its removal of ‘Palestine’ from displays
headlines.snippet: An investigation by Middle East Eye reveals that the British Museum made false claims about removing 'Palestine' from its displays. Internal emails show these changes resulted from complaints by pro-Israel activists.
